verses.commentary: Afterward Jesus appeared again to the disciples, by the Sea of Galilee. It happened this way. Third appearance (to group). Location: Galilee. Sea of Tiberias. Return to home territory. Epilogue begins.
Peter, Thomas, Nathanael, James and John (Zebedee's sons), two unnamed. Small group.

I'm going out to fish. I'll go with you. So they went out and got into the boat, but that night they caught nothing. Peter's initiative. Return to old profession. Others join. Night fishing. No catch. Futility. Back to beginning.
Jesus on shore. Unrecognized again. Distance or resurrection body.
He called out to them, Friends, haven't you any fish? No. Paidia (παιδία), children, lads. Friendly address. Fish question. Negative answer. Admission of failure.
Right side. Obedience. Immediate abundance. Cannot pull in. Overwhelming catch. Luke 5 parallel.
It is the Lord! Peter's impulsive response. Wraps garment. Dives in. Eagerness. Can't wait for boat.
Net towed. Two hundred cubits: about 90 meters. Near shore. Heavy load.
Anthrakia (ἀνθρακιά). Fish already cooking. Bread provided. Jesus prepares meal. Provision and hospitality.
Their labor valued. Participation invited.
Specific count. Large fish. Net intact. Symbolism debated. Completeness? Gentile nations? Unbroken net: unity preserved despite abundance.
Aristaō (ἀριστάω), eat breakfast. No one asks identity. They know. Certainty without questioning.
Took, gave. Feeding miracle echoes. Host serves. Provision continues.
Individual appearances not counted. Resurrection established. Multiple witnesses.

Peter addressed. Full name: Simon son of John. Question: Agapaō (ἀγαπάω), supreme love. More than these: other disciples or fishing. Peter responds: Phileō (φιλέω), affection. You know: appeal to Jesus' knowledge. Command: Feed lambs. Pastoral commission.
Agapaō again. Same response: Phileō. Command: Tend sheep. Poimainō (ποιμαίνω), shepherd. Leadership role.

This time Phileō. Jesus meets Peter's word. Peter hurt. Three questions reverse three denials. You know all things: omniscience acknowledged. Third command: Feed sheep. Full restoration. Triple commissioning.
Youth: independence. Old age: dependence. Stretch out hands: crucifixion. Led: arrested. Against will: execution.
Death glorifies God. Crucifixion prophesied. Follow Me: command repeated (1:43). Discipleship unto death. Peter will follow Jesus to cross.
John following. Identification: beloved disciple. Reference to 13:23-25. Last Supper intimacy recalled.
Comparative curiosity. What about John? Concern for another's fate.
If I want him to remain. What to you? Not your concern. Command: You follow Me. Personal obedience. Don't compare. Focus on own calling.
Rumor: John won't die. John corrects. Jesus didn't say that. Hypothetical, not promise. Clarification needed.
Beloved disciple wrote. Eyewitness testimony. We: editorial plural or church attestation. True testimony certified.
Many more acts. Selective record. World couldn't contain books. Vastness of Jesus' works. Gospel is sufficient, not exhaustive. Purpose achieved in what's written.
